title: Receive Data with Webhooks
lede: Use the Catch Webhook trigger in Automation to accept data from any outside system - so an incoming request can start a workflow, create records, or kick off a call.

steps.title: Add a Catch Webhook Trigger
steps.content:
1. Go to Intelligence Center → Automation and create or open a flow.
  2. Click the trigger block and choose Catch Webhook.
  3. iMash generates a unique webhook URL for this flow.

images.caption: Catch Webhook is the trigger that starts a flow from incoming data.
Catch Webhook is the trigger that starts a flow from incoming data.

steps.title: Copy and Send the URL
steps.content:
1. Copy the generated webhook URL.
  2. Paste it into the outside system (a form, another app, or your own code) so it sends a request to that URL.
  3. Trigger a real request once so iMash can capture a sample of the incoming data.

steps.title: Use the Incoming Data
steps.content:
1. After the sample arrives, the fields from the request become available to every step below.
  2. Add actions (for example a System Tables action to create a lead or start a call) and map the webhook fields into them.
  3. Test the flow, then Publish it so the webhook stays live.

steps.title: Sending Data the Other Way
steps.content:
1. Webhooks also work outbound: a Smart Agent can Send Data to URL during a call to push information to your systems.
  2. Set that up as a Custom Tool with a POST endpoint and attach it to the agent.

images.caption: Agents can POST data to your own URL mid-conversation.
Agents can POST data to your own URL mid-conversation.
